마가복음 4:1.content: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.

마가복음 4:2.content: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,

마가복음 4:3.content: “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,

마가복음 4:4.content: and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and devoured it.

마가복음 4:5.content: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.

마가복음 4:6.content: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

마가복음 4:7.content: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

마가복음 4:8.content: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”

마가복음 4:9.content: He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”

마가복음 4:10.content: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.

마가복음 4:11.content: He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,

마가복음 4:12.content: that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’ ”

마가복음 4:13.content: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?

마가복음 4:15.content: The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.

마가복음 4:16.content: These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.

마가복음 4:17.content: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.

마가복음 4:18.content: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,

마가복음 4:19.content: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

마가복음 4:20.content: Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”

마가복음 4:21.content: He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?

마가복음 4:22.content: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.

마가복음 4:23.content: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”

마가복음 4:24.content: He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.

마가복음 4:25.content: For whoever has, to him more will be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”

마가복음 4:26.content: He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,

마가복음 4:27.content: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how.

마가복음 4:28.content: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.

마가복음 4:29.content: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”

마가복음 4:30.content: He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?

마가복음 4:31.content: It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,

마가복음 4:32.content: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”

마가복음 4:33.content: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.

마가복음 4:34.content: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.

마가복음 4:35.content: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”

마가복음 4:36.content: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.

마가복음 4:37.content: A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.

마가복음 4:38.content: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”

마가복음 4:39.content: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.

마가복음 4:40.content: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”

마가복음 4:41.content: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”
